args.plan:
## 0) Subsystem Name Candidates (Vendored Marketplace Vibe)
Recommended default: **Depot** (simple, implies “vendored storehouse” more than “online marketplace”).

Good options (CLI noun in backticks):
- **Depot** (`loom depot …`): storehouse for vendored packs; clear install/update semantics.
- **Bazaar** (`loom bazaar …`): marketplace vibe; implies discovery/remote index (we can still do local-first).
- **Vendor** (`loom vendor …`): explicit about vendoring; a bit overloaded with “vendor” as verb.
- **Registry** (`loom registry …`): implies remote index + versions; heavier than we need initially.
- **Shelf** (`loom shelf …`): cute, library-ish; less obvious what it does.

Decision (default if you don’t pick): **Depot**.

## 1) What We’re Building
A new Loom subsystem that can **install, track, verify, and update** “packs” consisting of:
- OpenCode artifacts: `.opencode/agents/`, `.opencode/commands/`, `.opencode/skills/`, `.opencode/plugins/`.
- Optional repo-sidecar content (e.g. `_bmad/` workflows and config) that commands reference.

Goal behavior:
- `loom <noun> install <pack>` vendors the pack into the repo in a controlled, update-safe way.
- `loom <noun> update <pack>` re-syncs to the bundled version and warns on local edits.
- `loom <noun> status/doctor` verifies what’s installed and what drifted.

Non-goals for v1:
- No remote marketplace / downloading from the internet.
- No interactive TUI.

## 2) Pack Format (Local-First, Deterministic)
### 2.1 On-disk layout in Loom source
- `src/agent_loom/<noun>/packs/<pack_id>/pack.yaml`
- `src/agent_loom/<noun>/packs/<pack_id>/files/**` (tree copied into repo root)
- `src/agent_loom/<noun>/packs/<pack_id>/LICENSES/**` (attribution for vendored packs)

### 2.2 Pack manifest (`pack.yaml`)
Fields (draft):
- `id`: stable identifier (`bmad-method-compat` etc)
- `version`: semver-like string (or upstream tag)
- `description`
- `upstream`: opt... (truncated, len=7381)
args.summary: Design new pack/vendoring subsystem (name TBD) to install/update OpenCode agents/commands/skills, and vendor+adapt BMAD content for OpenCode with strong Loom ticket/workspace/memory integration and zero Claude-specific assumptions.

args.plan:
## 1) Naming
Subsystem name: **Depot** (`loom depot …`).

Rationale: reads as “vendored storehouse” and matches the behavior we want (install/update/verify packs).

## 2) What “Install BMAD” Actually Means (Traced)
BMAD’s installer is not just copying files; it *renders* an install from source:

### 2.1 Source inputs in the BMAD repo
- Module sources: `src/core/**`, `src/bmm/**`, etc.
- Agent YAML sources: `src/**/agents/*.agent.yaml`
- IDE templates (we care about OpenCode):
  - `tools/cli/installers/lib/ide/templates/combined/opencode-agent.md`
  - `tools/cli/installers/lib/ide/templates/combined/opencode-workflow.md`
  - `tools/cli/installers/lib/ide/templates/combined/opencode-task.md`
  - `tools/cli/installers/lib/ide/templates/combined/opencode-tool.md`
- Platform routing for OpenCode:
  - `tools/cli/installers/lib/ide/platform-codes.yaml` → installs to `.opencode/agent` and `.opencode/command` (singular)

### 2.2 Installer pipeline (what happens when you run it)
From `tools/cli/commands/install.js` and `tools/cli/installers/lib/core/installer.js` + `tools/cli/installers/lib/modules/manager.js`:
1) Create/refresh project-local `_bmad/` directory.
2) Copy module content from BMAD source into `_bmad/<module>/…` with filtering:
   - skips `module.yaml`, any `config.yaml` template, sidecar dirs, and `*.agent.yaml`.
   - copies workflows/tasks/docs.
3) Compile each `*.agent.yaml` into an installed **agent .md file** (BMAD calls it compiled XML-in-markdown) via `tools/cli/lib/agent/compiler.js`.
   - Important: compiler injects a party-mode menu entry referencing `_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/...`.
4) Generate clean installed `config.yaml` for each module with resolved defaults.
5) Generate OpenCode launchers:
   - Agent launchers come from “installed agents” discovery + `opencode-agent.md` template.
   - Workflow/task/tool commands come from artifact discovery + `opencode-*.md` templ... (truncated, len=8097)
args.summary: Add new `loom depot` subsystem to vendor/install/update OpenCode packs; vendor BMAD by *actually running its installer* into a temp project with OpenCode as the target, then post-process outputs to be OpenCode+Loom native and scrub all Claude-specific traces.

todos.content: Survey existing Loom CLI wiring and add `loom depot` command group
Implement depot core: pack discovery, manifest validation, install/update/status/doctor/uninstall, lockfile drift detection
Add BMAD vendor builder: run BMAD installer into tempdir with OpenCode target; collect/normalize outputs; apply de-claudify + loom-ify transforms
Create initial BMAD OpenCode pack resources under src/agent_loom/depot/packs/
Add tests for depot and BMAD pack invariants; run ruff, basedpyright, pytest

+from __future__ import annotations

+import hashlib
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Iterable
+
+
+def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
+    h = hashlib.sha256()
+    with p.open("rb") as f:
+        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
+            h.update(chunk)
+    return h.hexdigest()
+
+
+def sha256_text(text: str) -> str:
+    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
+
+
+def norm_rel_path(rel: str) -> str:
+    # Normalize to posix-ish relative paths for lockfiles.
+    return str(rel).replace("\\", "/").lstrip("/")
+

+    if not files_root.is_dir():

+    def _walk(base, prefix: str) -> List[Tuple[str, Path]]:
+        out: List[Tuple[str, Path]] = []
+        for child in base.iterdir():
+            name = str(getattr(child, "name", ""))
+            if not name:
+                continue
+            next_prefix = f"{prefix}/{name}" if prefix else name
+            if child.is_dir():
+                out.extend(_walk(child, next_prefix))
+            elif child.is_file():
+                with resources.as_file(child) as p:
+                    out.append((next_prefix, Path(p)))
+        return out
+
+    out = _walk(files_root, "")
+    out.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
+    return out

*** Add File: tests/test_depot_pack_lifecycle.py
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import json
+from pathlib import Path
+
+import pytest
+
+from agent_loom.depot.core import install_pack, uninstall_pack, update_pack
+from agent_loom.depot.lock import lock_path
+from agent_loom.depot.packs import list_pack_ids, load_manifest
+
+
+def test_depot_lists_builtin_sample_pack() -> None:
+    assert "sample" in list_pack_ids()
+    mf = load_manifest("sample")
+    assert mf.id == "sample"
+
+
+def test_depot_install_writes_files_and_lock(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    repo = tmp_path
+    res = install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False)
+    assert res.ok is True
+    assert (repo / ".opencode" / "commands" / "depot-sample.md").exists()
+
+    lp = lock_path(repo)
+    assert lp.exists()
+    doc = json.loads(lp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    assert doc["version"] == 1
+    assert any(p["id"] == "sample" for p in doc["packs"])
+
+
+def test_depot_update_respects_drift_without_force(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    repo = tmp_path
+    install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False)
+
+    p = repo / ".opencode" / "commands" / "depot-sample.md"
+    p.write_text("drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+    res = update_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False, force=False)
+    assert p.read_text(encoding="utf-8") == "drift\n"
+    assert ".opencode/commands/depot-sample.md" in res.drifted
+    assert ".opencode/commands/depot-sample.md" in res.skipped
+
+
+def test_depot_update_overwrites_drift_with_force(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    repo = tmp_path
+    install_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False)
+
+    p = repo / ".opencode" / "commands" / "depot-sample.md"
+    p.write_text("drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+    res = update_pack(repo_root=repo, pack_id="sample", dry_run=False, force=True)
+    assert "drift" not in p.r... (truncated, len=3128)

*** Add File: src/agent_loom/depot/vendor_bmad.py
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import re
+import shutil
+import subprocess
+import tempfile
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Iterable, List, Optional, Sequence
+
+from agent_loom.depot.util import norm_rel_path
+
+
+_TEXT_EXTS = {
+    ".md",
+    ".markdown",
+    ".txt",
+    ".yaml",
+    ".yml",
+    ".xml",
+    ".json",
+}
+
+
+def _run(cmd: Sequence[str], *, cwd: Path, timeout_s: int = 15 * 60) -> None:
+    subprocess.run(
+        list(cmd),
+        cwd=str(cwd),
+        check=True,
+        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
+        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
+        text=True,
+        timeout=timeout_s,
+    )
+
+
+def _is_text_file(p: Path) -> bool:
+    return p.suffix.lower() in _TEXT_EXTS
+
+
+def _read_text(p: Path) -> str:
+    return p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
+
+
+def _write_text(p: Path, text: str) -> None:
+    p.write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def _delete_if_exists(p: Path) -> None:
+    if not p.exists():
+        return
+    if p.is_dir():
+        shutil.rmtree(p)
+    else:
+        p.unlink()
+
+
+def _insert_after_frontmatter(text: str, insert: str) -> str:
+    # If file starts with YAML frontmatter, inject immediately after it.
+    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
+        return insert + text
+    # Find end of frontmatter.
+    idx = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
+    if idx == -1:
+        return insert + text
+    end = idx + len("\n---\n")
+    return text[:end] + "\n" + insert + text[end:]
+
+
+def _ensure_block(text: str, *, begin: str, end: str, body: str) -> str:
+    if begin in text and end in text:
+        return text
+    block = "\n".join([begin, body.rstrip(), end, ""]) + "\n"
+    return _insert_after_frontmatter(text, block)
+
+
+def _loom_integration_block(kind: str) -> str:
+    # Keep this short but act... (truncated, len=9883)

+                end="<!-- END:loom:integration -->",
+                body=_loom_integration_block("compiled-agent"),
+            )
*** End Patch

args.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: tests/test_depot_bmad_pack_invariants.py
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import re
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from agent_loom.depot.packs import iter_pack_files, load_manifest
+
+
+def test_bmad_pack_manifest_loads() -> None:
+    mf = load_manifest("bmad-method-opencode")
+    assert mf.id == "bmad-method-opencode"
+    assert mf.install_roots
+
+
+def test_bmad_pack_contains_expected_opencode_commands() -> None:
+    files = dict(iter_pack_files("bmad-method-opencode"))
+    assert ".opencode/commands/bmad-help.md" in files
+    assert ".opencode/agents/bmad-agent-bmm-dev.md" in files
+    assert "_bmad/bmm/agents/dev.md" in files
+
+
+def test_bmad_pack_has_no_claude_traces() -> None:
+    forbidden = [
+        re.compile(r"\bClaude\b", re.IGNORECASE),
+        re.compile(r"anthropic", re.IGNORECASE),
+        re.compile(r"\.claude/"),
+        re.compile(r"claude-code", re.IGNORECASE),
+    ]
+
+    for rel, p in iter_pack_files("bmad-method-opencode"):
+        ext = Path(rel).suffix.lower()
+        if ext not in {".md", ".txt", ".yaml", ".yml", ".xml", ".json"}:
+            continue
+        text = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
+        for rx in forbidden:
+            assert not rx.search(text), f"forbidden {rx.pattern} in {rel}"
+
*** End Patch

instinct_candidates.action: Do only inspection/analysis; produce a concrete, executable plan; call `submit_plan` with plan+summary; do not edit files or run write-capable tools until the plan is approved
instinct_candidates.title: Submit plan and wait for approval before implementation
instinct_candidates.trigger: When operating under Plan Mode / read-only constraints or when a plan is explicitly requested as a gate before coding

instinct_candidates.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` then `uv run ruff check .` and address issues; only then run `uv run pytest`
instinct_candidates.title: Run typecheck + lint gates before tests
instinct_candidates.trigger: After making Python code changes (or before declaring work done) in Loom

instinct_candidates.action: Run `git status`, `git diff` (staged+unstaged), and `git log -n ...` early to understand scope/style; avoid staging secrets and avoid destructive git operations unless explicitly requested
instinct_candidates.title: Git preflight: status + diff + log before commit/PR work
instinct_candidates.trigger: When asked to create a commit or a PR, or when preparing to stage changes
